name: External Mail Warning
author: Bulwark Mail Community
description: Warns before sending email to recipients outside your safe-domain list, and flags incoming messages from external senders with a banner. Admins can pre-populate organisation-wide safe domains.

safeDomains.label: Safe domains (organisation-wide)
safeDomains.description: Comma- or newline-separated list of internal/safe domains. Applies to every user. Subdomains match automatically (example.com also covers eu.example.com).

safeDomainsCsv.label: Additional safe domains
safeDomainsCsv.description: Comma- or newline-separated list of domains you trust (e.g. acme.com, partner.io). Subdomains are matched automatically.

treatIdentityDomainAsSafe.label: Trust your sending identity's domain
treatIdentityDomainAsSafe.description: If enabled, the domain of the From address is treated as safe automatically.

warnOnCcBcc.label: Warn about external Cc / Bcc
warnOnCcBcc.description: If disabled, only To recipients trigger the warning.

warnOnIncoming.label: Banner on incoming external mail
warnOnIncoming.description: Show a warning banner when an opened message has a sender outside your safe-domain list.

warnOnAuthFailure.label: Banner on failed authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, reverse DNS)
warnOnAuthFailure.description: Also show the banner when the message's Authentication-Results header reports a failure — even if the sender's domain is on your safe list. Strong signal of spoofing.

notifyOnIncomingExternal.label: Toast when external mail arrives
notifyOnIncomingExternal.description: Show a toast notification each time a new email arrives from an external sender.
